visual_features.overall_style: Modern, high-contrast, minimal layout with a strong editorial hero image. The dark top bar and large full-width hero create a bold, contemporary look appropriate for a nonprofit but slightly formal and corporate in tone.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, contrast, and positioning: (1) The full-width hero image with large white title text is the main focal point. (2) The black header bar with the site name on the left and navigation on the right forms the second level. (3) Within the hero, the single-line red/magenta underline beneath the title further emphasizes the headline. (4) Secondary elements such as the search box and WhatsApp action on the lower right are smaller and lower contrast, acting as tertiary focal points.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderately spacious. The header has generous vertical padding around the logo and navigation, and the hero headline has ample margin away from the edges of the image, providing breathing room. Elements are not crammed; however, the image and title occupy most of the visible viewport, giving a content-focused, editorial feel rather than a very airy minimalist one.
unique_elements: Full-width hero banner with large photographic background and centered/left-aligned white headline overlaid near the lower third
Thin red/magenta underline accent directly beneath the hero headline starting from the left, acting as a subtle brand/section marker
High-contrast black top header bar spanning the full width with logo text stacked on two lines
Floating WhatsApp chat-style circular button near the bottom-right corner
Clean, all-caps navigation items with generous letter spacing for a contemporary feel

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column responsive grid typical of WordPress themes, with content max-width likely around 1140–1200px inside full-width sections.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width top header with left logo and right navigation, followed by a full-width hero banner. Below (partially visible) appears a right-aligned sidebar area with search and floating action button, implying a main content + sidebar pattern.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top fixed/primary navigation header, then a hero/featured post section occupying the width of the viewport. Below, body content likely splits into a main article column and a right sidebar containing search and utility widgets.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header: left-aligned brand text and right-aligned horizontal nav within the same bar. Hero: headline appears horizontally centered within its text block but aligned to a left column of the grid within the hero image. Sidebar content (search label, input) appears left-aligned. Overall alignment follows a clean left-column alignment for most text and navigation for readability.
responsive_hints: Full-width hero image suggests it will crop/scale responsively across breakpoints.
Navigation is arranged horizontally, likely collapsing into a hamburger or stack on smaller screens.
The content + sidebar structure suggests sidebar may stack below main content on mobile.
The floating WhatsApp button is positioned relative to viewport and will remain anchored at bottom-right on different screen sizes.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: (1) A full-width, horizontally oriented header bar with logo text on the left and navigation links on the right. (2) Immediately below, a full-width hero section showing a photograph with the main headline overlaid slightly left-of-center horizontally and vertically around the lower third of the image. (3) Under the fold (partially visible), a main content area likely to the left and a narrower sidebar on the right containing a search widget, with the floating WhatsApp button overlapping the lower-right corner.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Within the viewport shown, approximately 20–25% of vertical space is allocated to the black header and its padding, and about 60–70% to the hero image and headline. The remaining visible space shows the start of the main content and sidebar (~10–15%). Horizontally, the header dedicates roughly 30% of width to the brand block on the left and 70% to navigation on the right. Below, the implied main content area would take about 65–70% width with a 30–35% sidebar on the right.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate density. The hero is visually rich due to the photograph but only contains a single line of text and one accent element, which keeps it clean. The header navigation is spaced and not crowded. The emerging sidebar content suggests more functional density below, but overall the design feels organized and readable rather than tightly packed.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye typically starts at the top-left logo text, then moves horizontally to the right across the high-contrast navigation (F-pattern). From there, visual weight drops diagonally down to the large white headline in the hero (Z-pattern completion). The red/magenta underline draws attention along the baseline of the headline. Finally, the eye is pulled towards the bottom-right corner by the high-saturation WhatsApp green button and the search box, creating a secondary focal area for interaction.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The primary visible button is the floating WhatsApp action: a circular button (~48–56px diameter) with a bright green (#25D366) solid fill, white WhatsApp icon centered, and likely a subtle drop shadow or elevation to separate it from the background. It sits a consistent margin (around 16–24px) from the bottom and right edges. Any standard CTA buttons (not visible here but consistent with this style) would likely be rounded rectangles with 4–6px border-radius, filled in accent color (magenta/red or green), white text, and possibly no border for a flat look.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Floating action button likely implemented as an anchor tag <a> with a fixed-position wrapper div for placement, containing an <i> or <svg> icon. Standard CTAs would be <button> or <a> elements styled as buttons, sometimes wrapped in a div for spacing and alignment in layout grids.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Although specific cards are not fully visible, the hero behaves as a large featured ‘card’: full-bleed background image with overlaid white text and a thin accent line. Typical content cards in this system would be simple rectangles with subtle border-radius (2–4px), a light drop shadow or light border (#DDDDDD), and internal padding of 16–24px. Imagery would sit at the top with text sections (title, meta, excerpt) stacked below, maintaining the same clean sans-serif typography.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure: a container div.card wrapping an image (img) or background-image on a nested div, followed by a text wrapper div.card-body with heading (<h2>/<h3>) and paragraph text. Optional meta info could be in a small, subdued text block above or below the title.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible search form shows a white rectangular input with a thin grey border (#DDDDDD), likely 4px border-radius, and internal padding (~8–12px). The label “Search” appears above the input in simple sans-serif text, left-aligned, with slightly smaller font size and darker grey color than body text. The form area itself is on a white card-like panel with a light border or shadow separating it from the page background.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms are probably laid out with labels above full-width inputs: <form> containing label + input pairs stacked vertically. Each pair is wrapped in its own div.form-group with margins between groups. The search may use <input type="search"> with an associated <label> for accessibility, and optionally an icon button appended or a submit button placed to the right or below.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal menu on a full-width black bar. Items are uppercase, evenly spaced with generous horizontal padding, and white text for maximum contrast. One item ('GET TO KNOW US') has a dropdown indicator, suggesting a hoverable menu. Active or hover states are likely indicated by text color change (to accent color) or an underline. The overall character is flat and modern, with no visible borders between items.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical implementation: a semantic <nav> containing an unordered list <ul> of <li><a> links. Dropdowns are implemented via nested <ul> inside parent <li>. CSS flexbox is used for horizontal alignment (space-between) of brand block and menu items. Active state is handled with a .current-menu-item or .active class on the corresponding <li> or <a>.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The navigation itself is a horizontal list with low density and high spacing. For content lists (like posts, though not visible here) the style would likely be vertically stacked, each item with clear separation via margin or subtle border-bottom line in a light grey. Any thumbnails would appear left with text to the right (media-object pattern), but given the cleaner aesthetic, list items are likely relatively spacious with clear typographic hierarchy (title bold, meta smaller and lighter).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Lists are expected to use semantic HTML lists (<ul>/<ol>) for navigation and structured div-based media objects for post lists: a container using flexbox with an optional image div followed by a text div containing title, meta, and excerpt. Separators can be implemented using border-bottom on list items or margin with background color contrast.

heading.visual_style: Bold, geometric/humanist sans-serif with a relatively high x-height, open counters, and clean straight lines. The headings and navigation are uppercase, giving them a contemporary, assertive feel.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif body text with good legibility and moderate stroke contrast, suited for both small labels and longer paragraphs.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most interactive components (buttons, inputs, nav items) should use 8–16px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding, consistent with the moderately spacious look of the header and form elements.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major vertical spacing between sections should range from 48–64px, with smaller internal groupings using 16–24px margins to keep sections visually distinct while maintaining cohesion.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely change color (to accent magenta or a lighter grey) and possibly show an underline on hover; active page may be distinguished by persistent color or underline.
Hero headline may be clickable as a link with subtle hover effect such as color change or slight opacity shift.
Buttons and the floating WhatsApp FAB may have hover states including slight scale-up (1.03x), shadow increase, or subtle background darkening to indicate interactivity.
Dropdown navigation for 'GET TO KNOW US' should use smooth fade/slide transitions (150–250ms) to maintain a refined feel.
Form fields can highlight on focus with border-color shifting to the accent color and light box-shadow or glow for accessibility feedback.
Scroll behavior may include a sticky header that remains at the top as the user scrolls, possibly shrinking in height for a more compact fixed navigation experience.
